experiences.intentions: There are two boxes: (2,1) is directly below the target at (1,1), making it an easy candidate to solve first by pushing it up. The player starts at (1,2), adjacent to the right of the top target. To push box (2,1) up, I need to stand below it at (3,1). That square is reachable by moving down from (1,2) to (2,2), then left to (2,1) is blocked by box, so instead continue around through open floor: down to (3,2) is wall, so the route is right/down/down/left/left/up? Better immediate step is to start moving toward the lower side via the open corridor on the right. No deadlock risk from simple movement. First move should be down to begin repositioning toward standing below box (2,1).
experiences.tasks: Push all boxes onto goal positions in the Sokoban puzzle.

experiences.intentions: There are two boxes and two targets. The box at (2,1) is directly below the target at (1,1), so the safest first objective is to push that box up onto the target. I am at (2,2), immediately to the right of that box, so I can push it left only into a corner at (2,0) which is a wall, impossible. To push it up I need to stand at (3,1), but that square is reachable by going down to (3,2) blocked by wall at (3,2)? Actually row 3 col 2 is '#', so I need a route around: from (2,2) go right through open area and loop down and left to reach below the box. The immediate best repositioning step is right to start that route and avoid deadlock. The other box at (3,4) should likely later go left/down toward target (4,2), but not yet. So move right.

experiences.intentions: The left box at (2,1) is directly below the target at (1,1). It is not deadlocked because pushing it up would place it onto a target, which is safe and desirable. The player is at (2,2), immediately to the right of that box, so can push left now—but that would shove the box into the wall at (2,0), a dead corner and lose the puzzle. Instead, I should reposition above or below? To push the box up onto (1,1), I need to stand at (3,1), but that square is currently reachable via going down from (2,2) to (3,2)? No, (3,2) is wall. Better long-term first move is to move right into open space to route around the central wall and approach the lower areas, avoiding oscillation and setting up access to (3,1) or the box at (3,4).

experiences.intentions: Targets are at (1,1) and (4,2). Box at (2,1) is directly below the top-left target, so it should be pushed up into (1,1) as soon as possible; that is safe because the destination is a target, not a dead corner problem. The other box at (2,4) can later be routed toward (4,2). Recent history shows oscillation, so the best move is to stop repositioning and make the productive push if adjacent. The player is most plausibly at (2,2) after the sequence ending with left from (2,3), which places them immediately to the right of box (2,1). From there, pushing left would be illegal because of wall at (2,0), but pushing up is not aligned; however the reflection indicates repeated failed push up near the other box, then left/down/left likely moved the player around toward the left box. The intended productive action is the push that places box (2,1) onto target (1,1): stand below it and push up. This is the only clearly goal-improving, deadlock-free action.

From player at (3,4), moving up to (2,4) places me directly left of the box, which may allow a later push left away from the wall—good progress and not a deadlock.

Immediate best non-push move is up from (3,4) to (2,4), placing me left of the box so next I can consider pushing right is bad, but from there I can navigate around via row 2/3 to set up a better sequence and stop oscillating vertically at current square.

experiences.intentions: The remaining box is at (2,5) against the right wall. It cannot be pushed right because of the wall, cannot be pushed up because (1,5) is floor but to push up I would need to stand at (3,5), and more importantly pushing it upward would place it at (1,5) still stuck along the wall with no target on that wall. It cannot be pushed down from current setup because that would require standing at (1,5), which is reachable, but pushing down to (3,5) also keeps it on the right side. The key useful move is to get left of the box and push it left off the wall eventually toward target (4,2). The player is already immediately left of the box at (2,4), and the cell beyond the box to the right is wall, so push right is impossible. A plain move is needed to reposition without oscillating. Best is move down to start routing around through the open lower area so I can later access (1,5) or other approach squares and work the box left/down safely. Avoid further up/down oscillation at (2,4)/(3,4), but among legal single moves, down at least progresses toward the lower corridor and target side.

But the cell below the box is the player at (2,4) adjacent diagonally, not directly under it; to affect the box I need to stand at (2,5). That square is reachable by moving right. From there, I can push the box down to (2,5), which is the only non-dead immediate option and may restore mobility toward the target at (4,2). So first reposition right to get under the box.
experiences.tasks: Push all boxes onto goal positions in the Sokoban puzzle.

experiences.intentions: The loose box at (1,5) is on the top wall and not on a target. The only way to make progress is to get to its left side and push it down, since pushing along the top wall is impossible and leaving it there risks permanent deadlock. Player at (2,5) can move down/right? Right is wall, up would push into wall, left begins repositioning through the open area toward the box's left/down access. This also stops the recent left-right oscillation by committing to a route around the central wall toward column 4 and then up to (2,4)/(1,4) access.

experiences.intentions: The remaining box is at (1,5) on the top row, not in a corner but trapped against the wall unless moved horizontally first. The only way to make progress is to get underneath it and push it left along row 1 toward column 2, where it can later be pushed downward to the target at (4,2). From the player's position at (2,4), moving right to (2,5) places me directly below the box, enabling a future push left from beneath/adjacent routing. This also breaks the recent left-right oscillation and repositions toward the active box.

To solve, it must first be pushed left along row 1, but that requires the player to stand at (1,6), which is impossible due to the wall. experiences.intentions: The unsolved box is at (1,5) on the top wall, and the empty target is at (4,2). To solve safely, first get the box off the top wall by pushing it downward, since leaving it on the wall limits options and risks deadlock if pushed into the corner at (1,6). The player is currently at (3,5), directly below open space at (2,5), so the immediate step is to move up to stand beneath the box. From there, a future push up is impossible, but being at (2,5) puts me adjacent below the box for a safe push down on the next turn. This also breaks the recent left-right oscillation and advances positioning.

experiences.intentions: The only unsolved box is at (1,5) on the top row. Target is at (4,2). Since boxes cannot be pulled, I should first get the box off the top row by pushing it down, then later guide it left and down toward the target. From the current player position (2,4), I am directly below-left of the box; moving up would place me left of the box but cannot help immediately, while moving right to (2,5) positions me directly under the box so a subsequent push up would be possible. This avoids oscillation and sets up the needed repositioning without risking a deadlock now.

experiences.intentions: The remaining box is at (1,5) on the top row, and the only empty target is at (4,2). Since boxes cannot be pulled, the first priority is to get the box off the top wall by pushing it downward. To push it down, the player must stand above it at (0,5), which is impossible because row 0 is a wall. Pushing it right is also impossible because (1,6) is a wall. The only feasible first box movement is to push it left from (1,5) to (1,4), which requires the player to stand at (1,6), also impossible because (1,6) is a wall. So the box is currently immovable from this state. Therefore, no push is available, and the best action is to reposition while avoiding oscillation. From the player at (2,5), moving left to (2,4) is the safest central repositioning move and may help assess routes without worsening deadlock.

experiences.intentions: The unsolved box is at (1,5) on the top wall. The only target is at (4,2). To solve, the box must first be moved left along row 1 away from the right corner area, then eventually pushed down toward row 4 and over to column 2. Right now the player is at (2,5), directly below the box, and the cell left of the box at (1,4) is empty floor, so pushing the box left is legal. Pushing right is impossible because of the wall at (1,6), and leaving the box on the top wall without action continues the oscillation. A push down is impossible from current position because the player is not above the box. Best immediate progress is to push the box left one step to (1,4), moving it away from the wall corner and preserving future routes.

For push up from (2,5), the box at (1,5) would go to (0,5), which is a wall, so impossible. Therefore no push is legal now. From player position (2,4), moving right to (2,5) is safe and is the first step to get below the box at (1,5), enabling a future push left if the geometry allows. This also breaks the recent oscillation and repositions purposefully without risking deadlock.

To make progress toward target (4,2), I need to get below the box and push it down off the wall. The player is already directly below the box at (2,5), and the cell below the player is irrelevant because a push up sends the box from (1,5) to (0,5) only if above were free, but row 0 is a wall, so that push is impossible. Since the box is on the top wall with a wall above, it is actually immovable from this position, confirming irrecoverable deadlock. I should avoid oscillation and choose a safe non-push movement to reassess, stepping left into open space.

experiences.intentions: The loose box at (1,5) is on the top wall. The target is at (4,2), so the box must first be pushed left along row 1 to column 2 or 3, then later pushed down toward row 4. To push the box left, I need to stand to its right at (1,6), but that is a wall, so left pushes are impossible from its current position. I also cannot push it right because it is already next to the right wall. Therefore the only productive first push is to push the box down from (1,5) to (2,5), which requires me to stand at (0,5) — impossible because row 0 is wall. Wait, reconsider coordinates: to push a box down, the player must be above it, so standing at (0,5) is indeed impossible. To push it left, player must stand at (1,6), also impossible. To push it up requires standing at (2,5), which is reachable, but would push into wall at (0,5), illegal. To push right requires standing at (1,4), but destination (1,6) is wall, illegal. This means the box at (1,5) is currently immovable and the puzzle state appears already deadlocked. Since only one action is required, avoid meaningless oscillation and move toward the box's left side at (1,4) to confirm constraints and avoid repeating left moves. From player (2,2), moving right advances toward the only relevant working area.

To move the box off the top wall safely, I need to stand below it and push down, but that requires the player to be above the box, impossible due to wall. So instead I should reposition leftward and examine a path to later approach; immediate legal productive push is not available. Current player at (2,5) can move left into open floor, which helps break the oscillation and approach the box from better angles via row 2/3. Moving left is safe and aligns with getting to the box's left side for eventual downward routing.

Since the box is above the player, the only legal push from here is push up, but that would hit the wall at (0,5), so illegal. Therefore I need to reposition to the left side or continue around to eventually get above? But because the box is on the top row interior, the only useful first move is to get leftward and around so I can later push it down from above? Wait to push box down, player must stand above it, impossible due to wall. So likely the only feasible route is pushing it left along the top row if targets/walls permit. From (1,5), left cell (1,4) is wall, so cannot push left either. This indicates the box is currently immovable from all directions except maybe from left pushing right, but right is wall at (1,6). experiences.intentions: The loose box at (1,5) is trapped against the top wall, so it cannot be pushed down because there is no space above for the player to stand. The only productive plan is to push it left along row 1 until it reaches column 2, then later push it down toward the target at (4,2). From the current player position (2,4), I should first move to (2,5), then on the next turn move/push from below? Actually to push the box left, I need to stand at (1,6), which is impossible because (1,6) is a wall. To free the box, I need to get it off the top wall, but that requires pushing it down from above, also impossible. So the box is currently irretrievably stuck unless the interpretation allows prior approach, but given the board the immediate best action is to stop oscillating and reposition away from the failed push area. Moving right to (2,5) is legal and may help inspect the corridor; other moves don't improve access. Choose a non-push movement.

experiences.intentions: The unsolved box is at (1,5) on the top wall, target at (4,2). A box on the top wall cannot be pushed down unless the player can stand above it, which is impossible due to the wall, so leaving it there is fatal. The only viable first step is to push the box left along row 1, because cells (1,4),(1,3),(1,2) are open and this moves it away from the right-side corner and eventually toward a column where it can later be pushed down. The player is at (2,5), directly below the box, so an immediate push up is legal and sends the box from (1,5) to (1,4) while the player moves to (1,5). This avoids oscillation and is the only progress-making action.

experiences.intentions: The remaining box is at (1,5) on the top wall. The only target is at (4,2), so this box must first be moved left away from the corner area, then eventually down and toward the target. To push the box left, I need to stand to its right at (1,6), but that is a wall, so left pushes are impossible now. Pushing up is impossible because of the top wall. The only productive immediate plan is to get below the box and push it down from (2,5). The player is already at (2,5), directly below the box, and the square beyond the box downward from the player's perspective is the box at (1,5) with destination (0,5) blocked for up; however for push down on a box above, Sokoban action semantics require the box to be below the player, so not valid. Therefore first reposition adjacent on the left side path to reach a useful angle; moving left begins a route around the open area to approach better and stops the recent oscillation.

experiences.intentions: The loose box is at (1,5) on the top wall. It cannot be pushed up, and pushing it right would jam it into the top-right corner, a deadlock since there is no target there. The only productive plan is to push it left along row 1 until it reaches column 2, then later push it down to the target at (4,2). To push left, I must stand to the right of the box at (1,6), but that cell is a wall, so pushing left is impossible. experiences.intentions: The remaining box is at (1,5), pressed against the top wall and near the right wall. It cannot be pushed up, and pushing it right is impossible due to the wall at (1,6). The only useful future plan is to move it left along row 1, which requires the player to get to the box's right side at (1,6), but that is a wall, so horizontal pushing from the right is impossible. The only other way to change box position is to push it down from (0,5), also impossible because row 0 is a wall. So the box is actually stuck permanently in a dead position. Since no push is possible, the best immediate action is to stop oscillating and reposition to inspect routes while preserving flexibility. Moving left opens space and avoids repeating right-left at the wall side.

experiences.intentions: The remaining box is at (1,5) on the top row, adjacent to the right wall at (1,6) and blocked by a wall at (1,4), so it cannot be pushed left or right from its current cell. The only way to free progress is to push it downward from below. The player is currently at (2,4), one step left of the needed pushing position at (2,5). Cell (2,5) is open, so the correct immediate repositioning move is right. This avoids oscillating aimlessly and sets up a legal push down next turn, moving the box from (1,5) to (2,5), away from the top wall and toward the target at (4,2).

experiences.intentions: The unsolved box is at (1,5) on the top wall, and the target is at (4,2). From the top wall, the first safe objective is to move the box left along row 1, because pushing it right would trap it in the top-right corner and pushing up is impossible. To push it left, I need to stand to the right of the box at (1,6), but that is a wall, so left-push is impossible from its current position. The only productive move is to push the box down from below: the player is already at (2,5) directly under the box, and (3,5) is open so the push is legal. This moves the box off the top wall into open space where it can later be maneuvered left toward column 2 and then down to the target, while avoiding oscillation and deadlock.

experiences.intentions: The remaining box is at (1,5) on the top wall, with walls at (0,5) and (1,6). It cannot be pushed left because the player cannot stand at (1,6), cannot be pushed right due to wall, and cannot be pushed up due to wall. It can only be pushed down from below if the player stands at (2,5), which would move it to (2,5) and free it from the wall. The player is already at (2,5), adjacent below the box, so the best immediate action is to push the box down to avoid continued oscillation and make future routing to target (4,2) possible.

experiences.intentions: The remaining box is at (1,5) on the top wall. Targets are only at (4,2), so the box cannot stay on the top wall forever; however it is not in a corner, so it can be moved downward if I stand below it. The player is already at (2,5), directly below the box, and the cell beyond the box upward is a wall, so push up is invalid. To make progress and avoid more oscillation, I should push the box down off the top wall into open space, which preserves solvability and starts a route toward the target on the left/lower area.
